page: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The physical hardware comprises 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. A key characteristic of this server is its memory-intensive configuration, offering 168.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 42.0 GB per core. It includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the server exhibits strong single-core capabilities, achieving a top-tier rating in Geekbench single-core (2355.0) and single-threaded gzip compression (34.91 GB/sec). Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 14.41 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is relatively limited due to the low core count, with stress-ng multi-core performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both falling into the weak tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small cached blocks (344.91 GB/sec) but weak performance for large uncached blocks (36.17 GB/sec). Database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, with Redis handling 6.31 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the FX8-4mds_v2 is its highly asymmetric resource distribution, pairing a massive memory footprint with a small CPU allocation. This makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads that require large memory capacities but do not demand heavy parallel processing, avoiding the need to provision excess vCPUs just to obtain high RAM. Conversely, it is poorly suited for compute-heavy tasks, as evidenced by its poor performance in large LLM inference and weak software compilation benchmarks. This server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, text processing, and lightweight web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmdsv2 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor clocked at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 168.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core, alongside 472 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, including strong Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores, though multi-core performance remains weak due to the 2 physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth is average for cached reads but drops for uncached blocks. This server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, caching systems like Redis, and text processing applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 168 GB of RAM, and 472 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high memory-to-core ratio server featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processors and local storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs clocked at 4.2 GHz
168.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB per core
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
